title: La Dame aux Camélias
review: Went with Alice, Macla and Ali
full_description: A moving and melancholy tale of impossible love. The story tells the 19th-century Parisian story of Marguerite Gautier, a young courtesan suffering from consumption, and a bourgeois young man Armand Duval. Choreography by John Neumeier to music drawn from Chopin's solo piano works and concertos. Neumeier weaves through La Dame a play-within-a-play of Manon that our protagonists watch, representing the spectre of betrayal by a courtesan.
ai_summary: John Neumeier's romantic ballet after Dumas fils — following a consumptive Parisian courtesan and her doomed love affair — choreographed to Chopin's piano music and framed with a devastating play-within-a-play. Dutch National Ballet performed it to nightly standing ovations.
